title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths · Changed-Topology Lab
summary: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a least-privilege management access plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed lab case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Site Client Vpn, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a least-privilege management access plan while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A site-to-site VPN automatically authorizes every device on both networks. This fails the Net Site Client Vpn contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives (N10-009 v4.0) — Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
